title: TIG Torch Assembly, Tungsten Sharpening, Stick Setup
summary: This section covers how to sharpen a tungsten electrode, assemble a TIG torch, and set up the welder for Stick welding. It includes safety requirements for grinding electrodes and step-by-step instructions for both TIG torch assembly and Stick cable connections.
key_facts: Dedicate a fine grit grinding wheel exclusively to electrode grinding to avoid contamination.
Pull electrode from the front of the torch; pulling from rear damages the Collet.
Snap off contaminated electrode end using pliers above the contaminated section.
Grinding direction must be parallel to the length of the electrode.
The conical tip portion should be 2-1/2 times as long as the electrode diameter.
Electrode must protrude 1/8 to 1/4 inch beyond the Ceramic Nozzle.
Collet and Collet Body sizes must match the Tungsten Electrode size.
Consult the Settings Chart on top of the welder for proper Tungsten Electrode size.
For Stick setup, plug Ground Clamp Cable into the Negative Socket.
For Stick setup, plug Electrode Holder Cable into the Positive Socket.
Twist both cable connectors clockwise all the way to lock in place.
The power cord plug will only fit into the Power Input Socket one way.

steps: 1. Shut off the welder and wait until Electrode and Torch have cooled enough to handle.
2. Remove Back Cap. Pull Electrode from front of Torch.
3. If Electrode is contaminated, use pliers to grip above contaminated section and snap off the end.
4. Lightly press Electrode tip against the grinding wheel at an angle; rotate until a blunt point is formed. Grinding direction must be parallel to length of Electrode.
5. The conical portion of the ideal tip will be 2-1/2 times as long as the Electrode diameter.
6. Re-insert Electrode into Collet with tip protruding 1/8"-1/4" beyond the Ceramic Nozzle, then re-tighten the Back Cap.
1. Consult Settings Chart on top of welder to determine proper Tungsten Electrode size.
2. Match Collet and Collet Body sizes to Tungsten Electrode size.
3. Thread Collet Body into the front of the Torch.
4. Make sure Ceramic Nozzle size is appropriate for application.
5. Thread Ceramic Nozzle onto Collet Body.
6. Insert Collet into back of Torch and into Collet Body.
7. Insert Tungsten Electrode into Collet on front of Torch.
8. Lock Electrode in place with Back Cap. Electrode should protrude 1/8" to 1/4" beyond the Ceramic Nozzle.
1. Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative Socket. Twist clockwise all the way to lock in place.
2. Plug Electrode Holder Cable into Positive Socket. Twist clockwise all the way to lock in place.
1. Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power Cord into Power Input Socket. Note: Plug will only fit one way.

warnings: WARNING! TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY: Some Electrodes may have materials added to them that are hazardous to breathe. Wear a respirator and ANSI-approved Safety goggles when grinding an Electrode.
WARNING! TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY FROM ACCIDENTAL OPERATION: Do not plug cord into wall outlet at this time.
